channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump taps immigration hard-liner Kari Lake as head of Voice of America
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he’s picking Kari Lake as director of Voice of America, installing a staunch loyalist who ran unsuccessfully for Arizona governor and a Senate seat to head the congressionally funded broadcaster that provides independent news reporting around the world.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House passes nearly $1 trillion defense spending bill, adding to US debt of $36 trillion
item.description: The U.S. House passed a mostly bipartisan bill to spend nearly $1 trillion on national defense, adding to the already growing $36 trillion national debt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces more nominations, including Kari Lake as director of Voice of America broadcast
item.description: President-elect Trump nominated Kari Lake to be director of the Voice of America, and Dr. Peter Lamelas and Daniel Newlin as ambassadors to Argentina and Colombia, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gov. Greg Abbott signals support for David Cook over Dustin Burrows in GOP fight for House speaker
item.description: Days after the Texas House GOP caucus backed Cook, Abbott made clear that he expects Republican representatives to do the same.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Juan Salinas Ii And Renzo Downey, KPRC 2

item.title: Lombardo joins GOP governors 'fully committed' to Trump deportation threats
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo and 25 other Republican governors lined up in support of President-elect Donald Trump's immigration policies on Wednesday, calling for the deportation of "individuals who pose a threat.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: The Trump and Biden teams insist they're working hand in glove on foreign crises
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump doesn't think much of Joe Biden's foreign policy record. The Republican president-elect frequently casts the outgoing Democratic president as a feckless leader who shredded American credibility around the world during his four-year term.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers edge closer to deal on government funding
item.description: Lawmakers say they are edging closer to a deal to keep the government's lights on in nine days but are still trying to work out differences over disaster aid and potential add-ons to the bill. There’s some optimism in the Capitol that bill text could arrive as soon as this weekend as the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House approves $895B defense bill with military pay raise, ban on transgender care for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House on Wednesday passed a $895 billion measure that authorizes a 1% increase in defense spending this fiscal year and would give a double-digit pay raise to about half of the enlisted service members in the military.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic senators urge Biden to act on temporary protections for migrants
item.description: The senators have been quietly urging the White House to take executive actions that would attempt to extend legal protections for migrants into Trump's administration, and the White House has been discussing what steps to take.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, COLLEEN LONG and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mace, activist dispute handshake that resulted in arrest
item.description: A disputed handshake is at the center of a Tuesday incident involving Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) that resulted in an Illinois man’s arrest, according to a police report and a witness who spoke to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth meets with moderate Sen. Collins as he lobbies for key votes in the Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the Defense Department, said he had a “wonderful conversation” with Maine Sen. Susan Collins on Wednesday as he pushed to win enough votes for confirmation.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border sheriff ignores county's new policy that blocks cooperation with ICE immigration enforcement
item.description: San Diego County Sheriff Kelly Martinez has said her office won't change its practices on ICE cooperation despite a new resolution passed by the board of supervisors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Wray resignation 'great day for America,' touts Kash Patel as 'most qualified' to lead FBI
item.description: President-elect Trump said FBI Director Christopher Wray's resignation is a “great day for America," while touting his nominee Kash Patel as the “most qualified" to lead the bureau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mast blasts Blinken over 'tens of billions' of US taxpayer dollars sent to Taliban post-Afghanistan withdrawal
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken was grilled by the incoming chair of the House Foreign Relations Committee on "billions" of U.S. dollars sent to Taliban.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries says he's 'prepared to find common ground' with Trump next year
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries said addressing the high cost of living for Americans could be an area of bipartisan cooperation next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, team trail Fauci on trust as medical information source: Survey
item.description: The latest poll from Axios/Ipsos American Health Index shows that 45 percent of Americans say they either have “a great deal” or “a fair amount” of trust in information regarding health topics coming from Fauci.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court dismisses Nvidia’s appeal in securities lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday dismissed an appeal from Nvidia in a securities lawsuit against the chipmaking giant, allowing the case to move forward. The court dismissed the appeal as “improvidently granted” in an unsigned, one-sentence opinion without explanation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Rising US-Mexico tension a game of optics, analyst says
item.description: Change is coming to the border on Jan. 20, when President-elect Trump takes office. How profound and lasting such change might be remains to be seen, but one thing is certain: Optics will play a big role in Washington, D.C., and in Mexico City.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Judge considering delay request in Routh attempted assassination trial
item.description: Attorneys for Ryan Routh, accused of attempting to assassinate Donald Trump, are requesting a 10-month trial delay
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, team trail Fauci on trust as medical information source: Survey
item.description: Trump and other Republicans have criticized Fauci, who was the face of the first Trump and Biden administration’s response against COVID-19, over his handling of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Trump names former CIA official Ron Johnson as ambassador to Mexico
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday announced his one-time ambassador to El Salvador, Ron Johnson, would serve as ambassador to Mexico in his upcoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps FTC Commissioner Andrew Ferguson to chair agency
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped Andrew Ferguson, a commissioner at the Federal Trade Commission (FTC), as the next head of the independent agency.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth makes play to win hard-to-get GOP centrists
item.description: Pete Hegseth is looking to win over a pair of key Republican moderates as he tries to keep his hopes of leading the Defense Department from being derailed. The former Fox News host huddled with Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-Alaska) on Tuesday and is set to do the same on Wednesday with Sen.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ bill looks to make doxxing a crime
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - When someone is doxxed, it can be more than just a leak of their information; it can rob someone of their well-being, physically and mentally.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine (R) announced Monday he was introducing a bill to limit college tuition aid to undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Report on attempts to kill Trump urges Secret Service to limit protection of foreign leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A congressional task force investigating the attempts to kill Donald Trump during his presidential campaign is recommending changes to the Secret Service, including protecting fewer foreign leaders during the height of election season and considering moving the agency out of the Ho
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: US says it will support new Syrian leaders who protect women and renounce terrorism
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday it will recognize and support a new Syrian government that renounces terrorism, destroys chemical weapons stocks and protects the rights of minorities and women. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said in a statement that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House task force cites 'various failures' ahead of 'preventable' Trump shooting
item.description: The House select committee that investigated the two assassination attempts against President-elect Trump released a report on Tuesday that found the July 13 attack at Trump’s rally in Butler, Pa., was “preventable and should not have happened.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kash Patel FBI plans spark concern over agency independence, retribution
item.description: Kash Patel hasn’t offered specifics on his plans for the FBI, but he’s made it clear that sweeping changes are on the table.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Patel FBI plans spark concern over agency independence, retribution
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, has promised to deliver a massive reform of a bureau he sees as among the agencies that have “weaponized the government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
